page: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by the AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps for external storage and network communication.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves a top-tier read-only bandwidth of 25059.68 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 657.74 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The platform also excels in database operations, static web serving throughput at 113.03 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations, which reach 331.8 million operations per second. Cryptographic and extended instruction capabilities are supported at the hardware level by AVX-512 and AES instruction sets.

While the bare-metal architecture offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage for data-intensive workloads. Qualitatively, the server provides high cost efficiency for compute-heavy and memory-bandwidth-sensitive deployments that require direct hardware access. This configuration is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: The Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server equipped with an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It delivers 192 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, the server relies on a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external data transfers. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput, which reaches 113.03 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation and cryptographic execution. The m7a.metal-48xl is highly suited for demanding workloads such as large-scale database operations, CPU-based LLM prompt processing, and high-throughput web serving where direct hardware access and high memory bandwidth are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 4 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, and web serving
